text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy village by the sea, there lived a young writer named Lily. Every evening after finishing her chores, she would glide down to the sandstone cliffs overlooking the azure ocean waves. Her imagination knew no bounds. One rainy afternoon, she decided to pen down tales of mystical sea creatures.

She dreamed up an old catfish named Flake who knew how to weave magical nets. Every night around moonlight hours, Flake would glide silently through seaweed. He'd catch slippery herrings and swirl them into enchanted threads.

One day, talking owl owl owl/owl-loving ducks came to visit. They wanted tales as good as Flake's nets!

Lily spun tales so vivid that stars seemed to sparkle. Sea unicorns rode waves upon azure horns. Sea lions had book clubs. And so did dolphins.

Soon villagers heard of these fantastic stories. Children snatched shells to hear owl owl owl/owl stories read aloud.
